location.office_time_required: 4-5 days per week when facilitating new hire onboarding or executing internal events

secondary_archetype: People Experience / Internal Events Program Manager

level_signals: 2+ years experience in an operational role required
Individual contributor owning end-to-end events delivery

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 2+ years experience in an operational role, preferably in a fast-paced or dynamic environment.
Ability and willingness to be onsite 4-5 days per week when facilitating new hire onboarding or executing internal events.
Ability and willingness to travel between global offices, up to 25% of the time.
Primary operational owner of global internal events, from vision-setting and budget allocation to vendor management and on-the-ground execution.
Strong organizational and problem-solving skills, with the ability to anticipate challenges, manage competing priorities, and make sound tradeoffs in a fast-moving environment.
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with experience collaborating across functions, both internal and external.
Strong attention to detail and a habit of maintaining accurate records, tracking project metrics, and surfacing the right information to the right stakeholders at the right time.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 2+ years experience in an operational role, preferably in a fast-paced or dynamic environment.
Occasional facilitation of new hire and intern onboarding.
A creative approach to project management built around lightweight frameworks, rapid iteration, and low-overhead communication that keeps partnering teams informed.

scope.surface: Internal events, people experience programming, new hire onboarding, employer branding, and external events across global Palantir offices
primary_metrics_inferred: Event execution quality and attendee experience
core_responsibilities: Primary operational owner of global internal events, from vision-setting and budget allocation to vendor management and on-the-ground execution
Design events and programming that build genuine community and connection across our people, while reinforcing Palantir's mission and values
Contribute to a wide range of operational workflows across the People Experience team, from Affinity and Interest Group programming to swag management and ad-hoc projects
Occasional facilitation of new hire and intern onboarding
Work directly with external vendors and internal teams to manage the full lifecycle of event logistics
Identify and onboard new vendors and tools to expand the team's capabilities and scale its reach

compensation.equity_form: Restricted Stock Units (RSUs), sign-on bonus and other potential future incentives mentioned

green_flags: RSUs and sign-on bonus mentioned as additional compensation
Comprehensive benefits package described in detail
Role has clear ownership and broad scope with explicit cross-functional partners listed
Explicit acknowledgment that 'the role and org aren't rigidly defined, and that's by design' signals growth opportunity
red_flags: No specific city listed for the role despite being onsite-required — candidates cannot confirm their commute requirement
Onsite 4-5 days per week requirement is effectively full-time in-office, limiting flexibility significantly
Up to 25% travel required in addition to onsite presence adds meaningful lifestyle constraint
Role scope described as deliberately broad and ambiguous, which may not suit all candidates

fields_inferred_not_stated: scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated in JD)
location.cities_listed (no specific city named for this role in JD body)
ambiguities: No city or region is specified for the role despite an onsite requirement; candidates cannot determine the office location from the JD alone.

The 'preferably in a fast-paced or dynamic environment' qualifier on the 2+ years requirement is technically a soft preference attached to a hard requirement — the 2+ years is treated as hard, the environment qualifier as soft.
Facilitation is listed under Core Responsibilities as 'occasional' but is also listed under What We Require as an expected capability — treated as a nice-to-have given 'occasional' framing.
Visa sponsorship not mentioned anywhere in the JD; marked as 'unclear'.
